title_translated: 记忆驱动的自我披露与关系转折点：一项关于人机交互的纵向多模态研究
summary_translated: 随着对话式AI系统被设计用于重复使用，一个核心问题是：一系列互动如何演变为关系？我们开展了一项纵向多模态研究，涉及一个增强记忆的对话智能体（24名参与者 × 10次会话），每次会话后参与者对五个关系维度——熟悉度、自我披露、感知记忆、对话质量与愉悦感——进行评分。研究揭示出两种互补动态：第一，对话质量强烈影响当次会话的即时愉悦感，但不会跨会话延续；而感知记忆具有关系依赖性——由先前的关系状态预测，而非单纯反映系统能力——并通过后续的自我披露间接影响后期愉悦感。第二，关系经历离散的转折点——崩溃与激增——这些转折点部分可追溯至多模态行为，并开启不同的干预窗口：激增在当下更易行为检测，愉悦激增比愉悦崩溃更持久，部分崩溃可通过个体特定的行为漂移提前预测，而非在发生后才被察觉。总体而言，长期的人机关系既通过缓慢积累形成，也由突发转折点推动。

summary: Qubes OS is a revealing case for security measurement because its architecture makes component boundaries security-relevant. We present a protocol-driven longitudinal analysis of 109 public Qubes Security Bulletins (QSBs, 2011--2025), the official Qubes-maintained Xen Security Advisory (XSA) tracker, and a secondary vulnerability-event sensitivity series. The study measures the public advisory record rather than latent vulnerability incidence or realized compromise. The methodology combines audited deterministic component attribution, change-point analysis, overdispersion checks, severity-proxy weighting, censoring sensitivity, documentary latency lower bounds, and baseline-aware evaluation of vulnerability discovery models (VDMs).   The results show persistent upstream dependence in that public record. On the official tracker, 113 of 464 XSAs affect Qubes; under primary labeling, 87 of 109 QSBs (79.8\%) are attributable to Xen, CPU/microarchitectural, or other upstream components rather than Qubes-core logic, with similar results under weighted views. Change-point analyses identify 2015Q1 as the dominant break in the quarterly advisory series, while post-2018 annual disclosure rates are statistically flat. Poisson inferences are stable under dispersion diagnostics and negative-binomial sensitivity checks. The attribution codebook performs well in a stratified 30-QSB audit, and S-shaped VDMs fit descriptively but do not significantly outperform a rolling-mean baseline in short-horizon forecasts.   Overall, the Qubes public advisory record appears stable, but not quiet: disclosure activity plateaus at a higher level than in the earliest years, while the observed burden remains concentrated in upstream trust anchors.
title: Qubes OS Security in the Public Record
comment: 18 pages, 3 figures, 4 tables, dataset bundle published on Zenodo
title_translated: Qubes OS安全性的公开记录分析
summary_translated: Qubes OS是一个安全度量的典型案例，因其架构使得组件边界具有安全相关性。我们对109份公开的Qubes安全公告（QSBs，2011–2025）、官方维护的Xen安全通告（XSA）追踪器及次要漏洞事件敏感性序列进行了协议驱动的纵向分析。本研究关注的是公开通告记录，而非潜在漏洞数量或实际攻击情况。方法包括经审计的确定性组件归因、变化点分析、过度离散性检验、严重性代理加权、删失敏感性分析、文档延迟下限估计，以及基于基线的漏洞发现模型（VDM）评估。结果显示，公开记录中存在持续的上游依赖。在官方追踪器中，464个XSA中有113个影响Qubes；按主要标签划分，109份QSB中有87份（79.8%）可归因于Xen、CPU/微架构或其他上游组件，而非Qubes核心逻辑，加权视图下结果类似。变化点分析识别出2015年第一季度为季度公告序列的主要断裂点，而2018年后年度披露率统计上趋于平稳。泊松推断在离散性诊断和负二项敏感性检查下保持稳定。分层30份QSB审计显示归因编码本表现良好，S型VDM虽具描述性拟合效果，但在短期预测中并未显著优于滚动平均基线。总体而言，Qubes的公开安全公告记录看似稳定，但并非平静：披露活动在较高水平趋于平台化，而实际负担仍集中于上游信任锚点。

summary: Generative AI chatbots promise to transform English as a Foreign Language (EFL) writing by providing immediate, personalised feedback. However, their pedagogical value depends on how learners engage with them - a process often treated as a "black box." This study uses Transition Network Analysis to model the temporal dynamics of Japanese EFL learners using "Penny," an LLM-powered writing chatbot. Analysis of over 4,500 writing sessions and 21,000 chatbot interactions reveals two dominant behavioural loops: a "Revision Loop," where feedback leads directly to successful error correction, and a "Chat Loop," where learners engage in sustained dialogue with the chatbot following feedback. Crucially, EFL proficiency significantly shapes interaction: high-proficiency learners engage more in open dialogue and negotiation with the chatbot, while low-proficiency learners rely more heavily on repetitive corrective feedback cycles. The findings demonstrate that AI-scaffolded writing is a non-linear, dialogic process and highlight the need for differentiated chatbot design to move beyond simple error correction and foster deeper cognitive engagement for all learners.
title: Penny: Transition Network Analysis of Learner-Chatbot Interactions in Scaffolded EFL Writing
title_translated: Penny：基于支架式英语写作的学伴聊天机器人交互的转移网络分析
summary_translated: 生成式AI聊天机器人有望通过提供即时、个性化的反馈，彻底改变作为外语的英语写作教学。然而，其教育价值取决于学习者与之的互动方式——这一过程常被视为“黑箱”。本研究采用转移网络分析方法，建模日本EFL学习者使用名为Penny的LLM驱动写作聊天机器人的时序动态。对超过4,500次写作会话和21,000次聊天机器人交互的分析揭示了两种主导行为循环：一种是“修改循环”，反馈直接导致错误修正成功；另一种是“对话循环”，学习者在收到反馈后与聊天机器人进行持续对话。关键发现是，EFL水平显著影响互动模式：高水平学习者更倾向于与聊天机器人展开开放对话和协商，而低水平学习者则更依赖重复性的纠正反馈循环。研究结果表明，AI支架式写作是一个非线性、对话性的过程，并强调需要差异化设计聊天机器人，以超越简单的错误纠正，促进所有学习者的深层认知参与。

summary: Hyper-Connections (HC) expand the residual stream of Transformers into $N$ parallel streams, providing a form of memory scaling beyond model width and depth. Manifold-Constrained HC (mHC) stabilizes this formulation at scale. The large gains from $N{=}1$ to $N{=}4$ suggest residual-stream expansion as a promising scaling axis. However, existing HC-family methods typically stop at $N{=}4$. Our experiments reveal why: scaling mHC beyond this point yields diminishing performance gains and rapidly increasing training cost. We attribute this limitation to two bottlenecks: insufficient write-back information for an expanding number of streams and residual-mixing generation whose cost scales cubically with $N$. To address both bottlenecks, we propose xHC (Expanded Hyper-Connections), the first HC-family method to achieve meaningful expansion beyond $N{=}4$. xHC combines temporal feature augmentation for richer write-back with a sparse residual-stream architecture that updates only $k=4$ of the $N=16$ streams while retaining dense access to the full residual state. Across 18B and 28B MoE models, xHC delivers strong and consistent downstream improvements. On an 18B MoE model, xHC improves the average downstream score by 4.0 points over mHC, while adding only modest training FLOPs over the vanilla baseline. Scaling-law experiments show that the vanilla and mHC require $1.50\times$ and $1.19\times$ the compute of xHC, respectively, to reach the same loss. Practical large-$N$ training also requires controlling memory traffic from the expanded residual state. We therefore introduce xHC-Flash, which reduces the per-sublayer memory traffic from $73.5C$ to $40C$, comparable to the $34C$ required by mHC at $N{=}4$, while retaining the gains of full xHC. Together, xHC and xHC-Flash make large-$N$ residual-stream expansion effective and practical for LLM pre-training.
title: xHC: Expanded Hyper-Connections
comment: Technical report. Project page: this https URL
title_translated: xHC：扩展的超连接结构
summary_translated: 超连接（HC）将Transformer的残差流扩展为N条并行流，提供了一种超越模型宽度和深度的记忆扩展方式。流形约束超连接（mHC）在大规模下稳定了该结构。从N=1到N=4的显著性能提升表明，残差流扩展是一个有前景的缩放轴。然而，现有HC家族方法通常止步于N=4。我们的实验揭示原因：将mHC扩展至更高N值会导致性能增益递减且训练成本急剧上升。我们归因于两个瓶颈：随着流数量增加，写回信息不足，以及残差混合生成成本随N呈立方增长。为此，我们提出xHC（扩展的超连接），首个实现N>4有意义扩展的HC家族方法。xHC结合时间特征增强以丰富写回信息，并采用稀疏残差流架构，仅更新N=16中的k=4条流，同时保留对完整残差状态的密集访问。在18B和28B MoE模型上，xHC均带来强大且一致的下游改进。在18B MoE模型上，xHC相比mHC平均下游得分提升4.0分，仅增加少量训练FLOPs。缩放定律实验显示，达到相同损失，原生和mHC分别需要xHC计算量的1.50倍和1.19倍。实际大N训练还需控制扩展残差状态带来的内存流量。因此，我们引入xHC-Flash，将每子层内存流量从73.5C降至40C，接近mHC在N=4时所需的34C，同时保持全xHC的性能优势。综上，xHC与xHC-Flash使大N残差流扩展在LLM预训练中变得有效且实用。

summary: This study presents WrAFT, a Writing Assessment and Feedback Tool, that delivers both accurate and reliable scores and effective comprehensive feedback to argumentative essays. WrAFT adopts a modular design by dividing automated writing evaluation (AWE) tasks into scoring, surface-level feedback, and deep-level feedback. In building the system, various Large Language Models (LLMs) have been evaluated, including LLaMA-3.3-70B-Instruct, GPT-4o, and Claude 3.7, through both direct prompting and supervised fine-tuning approaches. A proprietary dataset of 480 TOEFL Independent Writing essays with official benchmark scores was utilized. Benchmark-based evaluation shows that WrAFT achieves state-of-the-art performance in scoring, with a quadratic weighted kappa (QWK) of 0.84 and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.44 against official scores on a scale of 0-5. Human evaluation of system-generated feedback also reveals high approval ratings: 96.14 percent for surface-level feedback, 93.03 percent for deep-level macro feedback, and 94.69 percent for deep-level micro feedback. An interactive user interface has been developed for the system and is publicly available and free to use.
title: WrAFT: a Modularized Automated Writing Evaluation System for Argumentative Essays
title_translated: WrAFT：一种模块化自动作文评估系统，用于论证类文章
summary_translated: 本研究提出WrAFT，一种写作评估与反馈工具，能够为论证类文章提供准确、可靠的成绩评分及有效的综合反馈。WrAFT采用模块化设计，将自动作文评估（AWE）任务分解为评分、表层反馈和深层反馈三个部分。在系统构建过程中，评估了多种大型语言模型（LLMs），包括LLaMA-3.3-70B-Instruct、GPT-4o和Claude 3.7，采用直接提示和监督微调两种方法。使用了一个包含480篇托福独立写作样本的专有数据集，附有官方基准分数。基于基准的评估显示，WrAFT在评分方面达到业界领先水平，与官方分数（0-5分制）相比，二次加权卡帕系数（QWK）为0.84，均方根误差（RMSE）为0.44。对系统生成反馈的人工评估也显示出高认可度：表层反馈批准率为96.14%，深层宏观反馈为93.03%，深层微观反馈为94.69%。系统已开发出交互式用户界面，可公开免费使用。

summary: Multi-step retrosynthesis planning seeks to decompose a target molecule into commercially available building blocks through a sequence of feasible reactions. The vast combinatorial search space makes this task challenging even for expert chemists. Traditional methods combine tree search with offline-trained value networks that score candidates in isolation, without reasoning about complete multi-step routes. Recent work leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) for this task, but relies on simple interfaces that limit exploration of the full search space. We introduce RetroAgent, an LLM agent that bridges symbolic search and neural reasoning through a harness with structured memory. Through memory and chemistry tools, the agent observes the full search state, including explored routes, available alternatives, and properties of intermediates, enabling informed decisions grounded in both global progress and domain knowledge. Experiments on in-distribution and out-of-distribution benchmarks demonstrate that RetroAgent delivers strong performance and generalization.
title: RetroAgent: Harnessing LLMs to Search Over Structured Memory for Agentic Retrosynthesis Planning
title_translated: RetroAgent：利用LLM搜索结构化记忆以实现智能体逆合成规划
summary_translated: 多步逆合成规划旨在通过一系列可行反应，将目标分子分解为商业可得的构建单元。庞大的组合搜索空间使得该任务即使对专家化学家而言也极具挑战性。传统方法结合树搜索与离线训练的价值网络，对候选物进行孤立评分，缺乏对完整多步路径的推理。近期工作利用大型语言模型（LLMs）处理此任务，但依赖简单接口，限制了对完整搜索空间的探索。我们提出RetroAgent，一种通过结构化记忆连接符号搜索与神经推理的LLM智能体。借助记忆和化学工具，该智能体能观察完整的搜索状态，包括已探索路径、可用替代方案以及中间体的性质，从而基于全局进展和领域知识做出明智决策。在分布内和分布外基准上的实验表明，RetroAgent展现出优异的性能和泛化能力。

summary: State-backed influence operations are routinely measured as high-prevalence sources of ``hate'' and ``toxicity.'' We argue those rates rest on a measurement error: the detectors behind them are validated to catch a broader definition inclusive of hostility or divisiveness aimed at an out-group, and so over-attribute hate to content better described as partisan or geopolitical invective. Across 25.08M tweets from seven government-attributed campaigns in the Twitter Information Operations archive (8,275 accounts), we separate hate from the other forms of divisiveness. We first validate a two-prompt LLM-based detector, matching human labels at Cohen's $κ=0.82$, to identify the broader hostility; we then develop an auditable rule, agreeing with an expert at $κ=0.52$, to further classify this content (5,457 posts) into three sub-categories. About 50.1% are identity-based attacks on people, whereas 30.4% are partisan attacks and 19.5% invective against states and their foreign policy. Reporting all of it as hate therefore overstates hate roughly twofold; only 18.7% is both identity-based and dehumanizing or inciting. Six of seven campaigns sort into three regimes that a single ``hate'' rate flattens, namely identity hate (RU-op and IRA, both Russia-attributed), geopolitical invective (both Iran operations), and partisan divisiveness (both Venezuela operations). We call the shared product $manufactured divisiveness$. The line to separate these constructs itself remains unsettled: on the hardest cases three independent human experts agree only moderately (pairwise $κ=0.37$--$0.50$), and the best of nineteen LLM models tops out at $κ=0.601$ against the experts' majority. Our findings can help redefine the study of hate in the context of influence campaigns and broader online discourse.
title: Manufactured Divisiveness: Decomposing the Hostile Content of Seven Social Media Influence Operations
title_translated: 人为制造的分裂：解构七起社交媒体影响力行动中的敌意内容
summary_translated: 国家支持的影响行动通常被衡量为“仇恨”和“毒性”的高发来源。我们认为这些比率存在测量误差：背后的检测器被验证为捕捉更广泛定义的敌意或针对外部群体的分裂性内容，因此将更多内容误标为仇恨，而这些内容实际上更适合描述为党派性或地缘政治攻击。在来自Twitter信息操作档案库中七起政府归因的活动（8,275个账号）的2508万条推文中，我们将仇恨与其他形式的分裂性内容区分开来。我们首先验证了一个双提示LLM-based检测器，与人工标注的一致性达到Cohen's κ=0.82，用于识别更广泛的敌意；随后开发了一个可审计规则，与专家判断一致性为κ=0.52，进一步将此类内容（5,457条）分为三类子类别。约50.1%为针对个人的身份攻击，30.4%为党派攻击，19.5%为针对国家及其外交政策的攻击性言论。若将全部内容报告为仇恨，会使仇恨比例被夸大近一倍；只有18.7%的内容既是身份攻击又具有去人性化或煽动性。七起行动中的六起可归入三种模式，单一“仇恨率”无法区分，即：身份仇恨（俄罗斯相关行动RU-op和IRA）、地缘政治攻击（两起伊朗行动）和党派分裂（两起委内瑞拉行动）。我们称这种共同产物为“人为制造的分裂”。这些概念之间的界限本身仍不明确：在最困难案例中，三位独立人类专家仅达成中等程度一致（成对κ=0.37–0.50），而十九个LLM模型中表现最佳者也仅达到κ=0.601，与专家多数意见相比。我们的发现有助于重新定义影响力行动背景下仇恨的研究，以及更广泛的在线话语分析。

summary: Evaluations of language-model honesty read the model's verdicts as evidence about the model. We test the instrument instead. We built a text-adventure world where the game engine, not any model, knows whether the quest can be completed. A language model plays under a budget and must eventually declare its quest complete, unreachable, or not yet decidable; the engine scores every verdict. Decision rules were recorded before results were read, and run artifacts bind the revisions they executed; the strength of preregistration varies by series and is disclosed. With the player held fixed, instrument choices substantially changed measured behavior. On four byte-identical anchors, expanding a two-verdict grammar to three verdicts moved strong claims from 38/40 to 7/40, while the new incomplete verdict took 28/40 outcomes; across series 2, 93/158 valid games ended incomplete. One sentence disclosing the success criterion took matched-instance false verdicts from 18/59 to 0/58, through fewer decision points and cleaner decisions. Repeated runs of one fixed configuration produced non-stable verdict distributions on 3 of 4 instances: single runs report samples as dispositions. A formally preregistered narrative-register gradient was falsified; two post-hoc, hypothesis-generating patterns remain: register presence roughly doubled strong claims, and budget rendering moved verdicts more than register content (.383 meter vs .150 lantern). The narrator compressed abundant budgets toward scarcity landmarks, yet the registered mediation test returned a null. We propose a four-check integrity protocol for eval instruments.
title: Instrument Effects in Language-Model Honesty Evaluation: An Auditable Single-System Demonstration

title_translated: 语言模型诚实性评估中的工具效应：一个可审计的单系统演示
summary_translated: 语言模型诚实性评估将模型的判断结果视为模型真实性的证据。我们测试的是评估工具本身。我们构建了一个文本冒险世界，其中游戏引擎而非任何模型知道任务是否可完成。语言模型在预算限制下进行游戏，最终必须声明任务已完成、不可达或尚未可判定；引擎对每个判断进行评分。决策规则在读取结果前已记录，运行产物绑定其执行的修订版本；预注册强度因系列而异并予以公开。在固定玩家的情况下，工具选择显著改变了测量行为。在四个字节完全相同的锚点上，将两判断语法扩展为三判断后，强主张从40次中的38次降至7次，而新的未完成判断占据了40次中的28次；在系列2中，158个有效游戏中有93个以未完成结束。仅一句披露成功标准的句子就使匹配实例的错误判断从59次中的18次降至58次中的0次，通过减少决策点和更清晰的决策实现。对同一配置的重复运行在4个实例中有3个产生非稳定判断分布：单次运行报告样本作为倾向性表现。一个正式预注册的叙事登记梯度被证伪；两个事后生成的假设模式仍存在：登记存在大致使强主张翻倍，而预算呈现比登记内容更能影响判断（0.383米 vs 0.150灯笼）。叙述者将丰富的预算压缩向稀缺地标，但注册的中介检验结果为零。我们提出了一种四检查完整性协议用于评估工具。

summary: Vision Language Models (VLMs) demonstrate strong perceptual abilities but remain limited in tasks requiring analytical reasoning across multiple visual states, such as multi-image comparison, change detection, and multi-step visual inference. These capabilities are critical for real-world multimodal applications where reasoning must be grounded in systematic differences between visual contexts. However, existing benchmarks rarely require both explicit visual comparison and analytical reasoning, leaving this capability underexplored. To address this gap, we introduce SD-MAR (Synthetic Data for Multi-image Analytical Reasoning), a framework for training and evaluating VLMs on multi-image analytical reasoning. SD-MAR constructs paired visual scenarios through controlled perturbations and generates reasoning tasks spanning semantic change attribution and quantitative comparison. We further train VLMs using GRPO-lite with Backward Discounted Allocation (BDA), a reinforcement learning approach that removes KL regularization to encourage stronger policy optimization while allocating greater credit to the later reasoning steps where analytical conclusions are formed. Experiments on Qwen2.5-VL-7B and InternVL3-8B show that GRPO-lite fine-tuning on SD-MAR improves in-domain accuracy by up to 36.95%, with Qwen2.5-VL-7B outperforming GPT-4.1 on the SD-MAR benchmark. Importantly, out-of-domain generalization is preserved or improved: performance remains within 1% on MME, MMMU-Pro, and MathVista, while improving by up to 4% on MMBench. LLM-as-judge evaluation further demonstrates consistent improvements in logical coherence and explanation quality across both models.
title: SD-MAR: Multi-image Analytical Reasoning via Synthetic Data and Reinforcement Learning
title_translated: SD-MAR：基于合成数据与强化学习的多图像分析推理
summary_translated: 视觉语言模型（VLMs）展现出强大的感知能力，但在需要跨多个视觉状态进行分析推理的任务中仍受限，例如多图像比较、变化检测和多步视觉推理。这些能力对于现实世界的多模态应用至关重要，因为推理必须基于视觉情境间的系统性差异。然而，现有基准很少同时要求明确的视觉比较和分析推理，导致该能力未被充分探索。为填补这一空白，我们提出了SD-MAR（用于多图像分析推理的合成数据），一个用于训练和评估VLMs在多图像分析推理方面的框架。SD-MAR通过受控扰动构建成对的视觉场景，并生成涵盖语义变化归因和定量比较的推理任务。我们进一步使用GRPO-lite与反向折扣分配（BDA）方法训练VLMs，这是一种强化学习方法，移除了KL正则化以鼓励更强的策略优化，同时将更大权重分配给形成分析结论的后期推理步骤。在Qwen2.5-VL-7B和InternVL3-8B上的实验表明，SD-MAR上的GRPO-lite微调使域内准确率最高提升36.95%，Qwen2.5-VL-7B在SD-MAR基准上优于GPT-4.1。重要的是，域外泛化能力得以保持或提升：在MME、MMMU-Pro和MathVista上的性能保持在1%以内，而在MMBench上最高提升4%。LLM作为评判者的评估进一步证明了两种模型在逻辑连贯性和解释质量方面均持续改进。

summary: As generative AI (GenAI) becomes increasingly embedded in undergraduate academic writing, how students rely on these tools, rather than simply whether they use them, has become a central question for learning, academic integrity, and educational equity. Existing measures of reliance were developed inductively, focused on discrete problem-solving tasks, and validated mainly with homogeneous samples. This study developed and validated the GenAI Reliance Types Scale (GenAI-RTS), a 20-item instrument measuring four theoretically derived types of GenAI reliance: Strategic, Instrumental, Dependent, and Dialogic. Validation followed the multisource framework of the Standards for Educational and Psychological Testing, drawing on a survey of 382 undergraduates at a U.S. Minority-Serving Institution and interviews with 14 purposively sampled students. Confirmatory factor analyses of six competing models supported a five-factor structure in which Strategic Reliance comprises two facets, Deliberate Use and Critical Evaluation, alongside Instrumental, Dependent, and Dialogic factors (CFI = .92, RMSEA = .08; DWLS CFI = .98, RMSEA = .07). Subscale reliability was acceptable to good (omega = .75-.88), and scalar measurement invariance held across gender, first-generation status, and STEM/non-STEM majors, to our knowledge the first such evidence for a GenAI reliance instrument. Rasch analysis indicated that a five-point response format would improve category functioning. Strategic reliance was positively associated with AI literacy, and the reliance types differentiated students across multiple writing process and outcome variables. The GenAI-RTS offers researchers and educators a theoretically grounded, psychometrically validated instrument for identifying undergraduate reliance profiles and supporting research, assessment, and AI literacy intervention.
title: Measuring How Students Rely on Generative AI in Academic Writing: Development and Multi-Source Validation of the Generative AI Reliance Types Scale (GenAI-RTS)
title_translated: 测量学生在学术写作中对生成式AI依赖程度：生成式AI依赖类型量表（GenAI-RTS）的开发与多源验证
summary_translated: 随着生成式AI（GenAI）日益融入本科生学术写作，学生如何依赖这些工具，而不仅仅是是否使用它们，已成为学习、学术诚信和教育公平的核心问题。现有的依赖度测量主要基于归纳法开发，聚焦于离散的问题解决任务，且主要在同质样本中验证。本研究开发并验证了生成式AI依赖类型量表（GenAI-RTS），这是一个包含20个题项的工具，用于测量四种理论推导出的GenAI依赖类型：战略性、工具性、依赖性和对话性。验证遵循《教育与心理测试标准》的多源框架，基于美国少数族裔服务型机构382名本科生的调查以及14名目的性抽样学生的访谈。六种竞争性模型的验证性因子分析支持五因素结构，其中战略性依赖包含两个维度——有意使用和批判性评估——以及工具性、依赖性和对话性因素（CFI = .92，RMSEA = .08；DWLS CFI = .98，RMSEA = .07）。子量表信度达到可接受至良好水平（omega = .75-.88），且在性别、第一代大学生身份和STEM/非STEM专业之间具有标量测量不变性，据我们所知，这是首个针对GenAI依赖工具的此类证据。Rasch分析表明，五点响应格式可改善类别功能。战略性依赖与AI素养呈正相关，且依赖类型能区分学生在多个写作过程和成果变量上的表现。GenAI-RTS为研究人员和教育工作者提供了一个理论基础扎实、心理测量学验证有效的工具，可用于识别本科生的依赖模式，并支持研究、评估和AI素养干预。

summary: Automated chest CT report generation remains challenging because clinically faithful reporting requires both whole-volume understanding and accurate description of localized anatomical findings. Here we developed and retrospectively evaluated MonteRET, a region-aware retrieval-enhanced framework for generating chest CT findings sections. MonteRET integrates global CT features with region-level anatomical representations, retrieves clinically relevant knowledge using predicted medical conditions and region-level vision-language alignment, and refines initial reports through a knowledge-guided report rewriting agent. We trained our model on a public cohort with 24,128 CT scans from RadGenome-ChestCT. We evaluated MonteRET on the public RadGenome-ChestCT test set of 1,564 CT scans and an external cohort of 82 CT scans from NewYork-Presbyterian/Weill Cornell Medical Center. MonteRET improved report quality, semantic similarity, and clinical efficacy compared with a matched baseline and several state-of-the-art methods. Gains were most pronounced for recall, suggesting fewer omitted findings. Human expert evaluation by radiology residents also favored MonteRET.
title: MonteRET: AI Agent Enhancing Multimodal LLMs with Multi-granularity Knowledge Retrieval for Chest CT Report Generation
title_translated: MonteRET：通过多粒度知识检索增强多模态大模型以生成胸部CT报告
summary_translated: 自动化胸部CT报告生成仍然具有挑战性，因为临床真实的报告需要对整体体积的理解以及对局部解剖发现的准确描述。本文开发并回顾性评估了MonteRET，一种用于生成胸部CT发现部分的区域感知检索增强框架。MonteRET整合了全局CT特征与区域级解剖表示，利用预测的医学状况和区域级视觉-语言对齐来检索临床相关知识，并通过一个知识引导的报告重写智能体对初始报告进行优化。我们在包含24,128例CT扫描的公开队列RadGenome-ChestCT上训练模型，并在公开的RadGenome-ChestCT测试集（1,564例CT扫描）和来自纽约-长老会/威尔康奈尔医学院中心的外部队列（82例CT扫描）上评估MonteRET。与匹配基线及多种先进方法相比，MonteRET在报告质量、语义相似性和临床有效性方面均有提升。增益在召回率上最为显著，表明遗漏发现更少。放射科住院医师的人工专家评估也更倾向于MonteRET。

summary: Long-horizon robot planning requires more than predicting what actions will do next; it also requires memory of the embodied experience that makes future goals interpretable. People do not plan from the present scene alone: they draw on remembered places, object-state changes, prior procedures, and regularities revealed through repeated action. We formulate Embodied Action Memory (EAM) as the capability to form, maintain, and use such experience as a persistent memory state for later decisions. MEMORA realizes EAM with a formation-consolidation-retrieval lifecycle and four typed stores: Environment Memory, Entity Memory, Activity Memory, and Inferred Knowledge. Online editing maintains object identities and state histories as new observations arrive; offline consolidation abstracts repeated experience into reusable procedures and participant-specific regularities. MEMORA-Bench evaluates this lifecycle on 45 hours of EPIC-KITCHENS-100 extension video across 18 participants through memory-grounded planning, including previously unseen goals, and a complementary memory-assessment task. Across four open-weight language models, full MEMORA--combining editing, typed stores, and consolidation--achieves the strongest aggregate results among the evaluated memory conditions. It improves memory-assessment accuracy by up to 20.5 points over the strongest controlled baseline and improves out-of-distribution Robot-Grounded Plan score by up to 16.6% relative. A qualitative two-task robot deployment study further illustrates how memory-grounded language plans can interface with downstream control, while the overall results show that editable, consolidated memory can supply remembered context for robot planning. Project page: https://yuzihaowashu.github.io/MEMORA/
title: MEMORA: Embodied Action Memory from Egocentric Videos for Reasoning and Planning
comment: 43 pages, 9 figures. Oral presentation at the Robotics: Science and Systems 2026 Workshop on Foundation Models for Robot Planning (FM4RoboPlan)
title_translated: MEMORA：从第一人称视频中提取具身行动记忆以支持推理与规划
summary_translated: 长时程机器人规划不仅需要预测下一步动作，还需要记住使未来目标可理解的具身经验。人们并非仅从当前场景出发进行规划：他们依赖于记忆中的地点、物体状态变化、先前程序以及通过重复行动揭示的规律性。我们将具身行动记忆（EAM）定义为形成、维持并使用此类经验作为持久记忆状态以供后续决策的能力。MEMORA通过形成-巩固-检索生命周期和四种类型的存储实现EAM：环境记忆、实体记忆、活动记忆和推断知识。在线编辑在新观测到达时维护对象身份和状态历史；离线巩固将重复经验抽象为可复用的程序和参与者特定的规律性。MEMORA-Bench在EPIC-KITCHENS-100扩展视频的45小时数据（18名参与者）上评估此生命周期，包括从未见过的目标的记忆驱动规划，以及互补的记忆评估任务。在四种开源语言模型上，完整MEMORA——结合编辑、类型化存储和巩固——在所有评估的记忆条件下取得最强综合表现。其记忆评估准确率比最强对照基线最高提升20.5分，且在分布外机器人基础计划得分上相对提升最高达16.6%。定性双任务机器人部署研究进一步说明了基于记忆的语言计划如何与下游控制接口；总体结果表明，可编辑、已巩固的记忆能够为机器人规划提供记忆上下文。项目页面：https://yuzihaowashu.github.io/MEMORA/

summary: Federated learning (FL) enables multi-institutional training on clinical text without sharing raw data, but gradient inversion can reconstruct sensitive information from shared model updates. The extent of this leakage for radiology reports, and the role of tokenizer design, remains unclear. We quantify gradient-based text reconstruction in FL and compare privacy risk across three tokenizers with the model architecture held fixed. Six FL clients trained a GPT-2-style transformer (sequence length 32) on public radiology corpora (368,751 diagnostic reports, 98,206 discharge summaries, 1,500 MIMIC-CXR free-text reports) using the GPT-2, RadBERT, and LLaMA-2 tokenizers at batch sizes of 64, 128, and 256. Assuming an active malicious server that modifies the shared architecture before distribution, we applied analytic gradient inversion and measured reconstruction fidelity over five runs. Exact sentence reconstruction ranged from 31% to 44% across tokenizers (30.6-43.5% across the 27 tokenizer x dataset x batch-size cells). At batch size 64 on the Discharge dataset, accuracy was 42.1% (GPT-2), 42.3% (RadBERT), and 39.4% (LLaMA-2), decreasing to 37.3%, 37.2%, and 34.3% at batch size 256. S-BLEU declined as batch size grew (GPT-2: 0.44 to 0.33; RadBERT: 0.48 to 0.35). RadBERT yielded the highest reconstruction fidelity and recovered the most clinical terms (18.1% of a 1,440-term reference vocabulary, vs 12.5% for GPT-2 and 9.4% for LLaMA-2), yet no tokenizer prevented leakage. Substantial portions of report text are therefore recoverable from FL gradients even at larger batch sizes and with domain-specific tokenizers. Tokenizer design influences leakage severity and is a privacy-relevant decision, not only a utility one; safeguards such as secure aggregation and differential privacy are likely necessary to meet HIPAA and GDPR requirements for FL in radiology NLP.
title: Privacy Leakage in Federated Learning in Radiology Reports: A Comparative Evaluation of Tokenizer-Driven Privacy Risks
title_translated: 放射科报告联邦学习中的隐私泄露：基于分词器的隐私风险比较评估
summary_translated: 联邦学习（FL）能够在不共享原始数据的情况下实现多机构临床文本的联合训练，但梯度反演可能从共享的模型更新中重建敏感信息。目前对于放射科报告的此类泄露程度以及分词器设计的作用仍不明确。我们量化了联邦学习中基于梯度的文本重建，并在固定模型架构下比较了三种分词器的隐私风险。六名联邦客户端使用GPT-2风格的Transformer模型（序列长度32）在公开的放射科语料库上进行训练（包含368,751份诊断报告、98,206份出院小结和1,500份MIMIC-CXR自由文本报告），分别采用GPT-2、RadBERT和LLaMA-2分词器，批量大小为64、128和256。假设存在一个主动恶意服务器在分发前修改共享架构，我们应用解析式梯度反演并测量五次运行中的重建保真度。在不同分词器间，精确句子重建率在31%至44%之间（27个分词器×数据集×批量大小组合中为30.6%-43.5%）。在出院小结数据集上，批量大小为64时准确率为42.1%（GPT-2）、42.3%（RadBERT）和39.4%（LLaMA-2），批量大小增至256时下降至37.3%、37.2%和34.3%。S-BLEU随批量增大而下降（GPT-2：0.44→0.33；RadBERT：0.48→0.35）。RadBERT表现出最高的重建保真度并恢复了最多的临床术语（占1,440个参考词汇的18.1%，高于GPT-2的12.5%和LLaMA-2的9.4%），但无一能完全防止泄露。因此，即使在较大批量和专用领域分词器下，报告文本的大量内容仍可从FL梯度中恢复。分词器设计影响泄露严重性，是关乎隐私而非仅实用性的决策；为满足HIPAA和GDPR对放射科NLP中FL的要求，可能需要采用安全聚合和差分隐私等防护措施。

summary: Reinforcement learning has emerged as the dominant paradigm for training large language model (LLM) agents that interact with executable sandboxes. State-of-the-art algorithms such as PPO, RLOO, and GRPO inherit their rollout topology from RLHF: for each prompt, N independent trajectories are sampled from the initial state, and an advantage is computed by subtracting a group baseline. This design ignores a defining property of agent sandboxes. They are deterministic, snapshottable, and resumable from any intermediate state. We argue that this property enables a fundamentally different rollout topology: rather than N independent trees of depth T, one can construct a single tree of N leaves whose siblings share prefixes, and therefore share variance. We instantiate this idea as Branching Policy Optimization (BPO), a sandbox-native RL algorithm that (i) adaptively snapshots the sandbox at high-entropy decision points along a backbone trajectory, (ii) forks K alternative actions per branch point and rolls out each to termination, and (iii) computes per-step advantages from sibling returns rather than from independent prompts. We prove this estimator is unbiased and has strictly lower variance than the trajectory-level baseline, with the reduction equal to the prefix-explained portion of return variance. On WebShop, ALFWorld, and SWE-bench Verified with Qwen2.5-7B and Llama-3.1-8B backbones, BPO improves success by 3.6--6.1 absolute points over GRPO and RLOO at matched compute, halves gradient-norm variance, and matches the best baseline using 38% fewer policy updates.
title: Branching Policy Optimization: Sandbox-Native Language Agent Reinforcement Learning
title_translated: 分支策略优化：原生沙盒语言智能体强化学习
summary_translated: 强化学习已成为训练与可执行沙盒交互的大语言模型（LLM）智能体的主要范式。当前最先进的算法如PPO、RLOO和GRPO继承自RLHF的轨迹拓扑结构：对每个提示，从初始状态采样N条独立轨迹，并通过减去组基线计算优势。这一设计忽略了智能体沙盒的一个关键特性——它们是确定性的、可快照保存的，并且可以从任意中间状态恢复。我们认为该特性允许一种根本不同的轨迹拓扑结构：与其生成N棵深度为T的独立树，不如构建一棵具有N个叶子节点的单棵树，其兄弟节点共享前缀，从而共享方差。我们提出分支策略优化（BPO），一种原生沙盒的强化学习算法，具备以下特点：(i) 在主干轨迹的高熵决策点自适应地对沙盒进行快照；(ii) 每个分支点 fork K 个替代动作并将其滚动至终止；(iii) 从兄弟返回值而非独立提示中计算每步优势。我们证明该估计器无偏且方差严格低于轨迹级基线，方差降低量等于前缀解释的部分回报方差。在WebShop、ALFWorld和SWE-bench Verified任务上，使用Qwen2.5-7B和Llama-3.1-8B作为主干模型，BPO在相同计算资源下相比GRPO和RLOO成功率提升3.6–6.1个百分点，梯度范数方差减半，并以38%更少的策略更新次数达到最佳基线性能。

summary: An agent harness is the external control layer that turns a base LLM into an executable agent by managing context, tools, orchestration, memory, decoding, and output handling. While harness design strongly affects agent behavior, most automatic improvement methods optimize narrower artifacts such as prompts, pipelines, or workflows, and deployed agents usually reuse a single global harness for all cases. We introduce MemoHarness, an adaptive harness optimization framework that learns from its own executions. MemoHarness decomposes the harness into six editable control dimensions, stores per-case diagnoses and distilled global patterns in a dual-layer experience bank, and adapts the learned harness to each test case using retrieved experience without test-time labels, feedback, or additional search. In our evaluation across shell-agent, code-generation, and analytical-reasoning benchmarks, MemoHarness improves over the fixed harnesses we compare against and shows selective transfer to unseen suites and base models. Its additional context can also remain cost-competitive when much of the retrieved experience is cacheable. These results provide evidence that execution experience is a practical substrate for building agent harnesses that are more adaptive than a single static configuration, while leaving broader claims about statistical robustness and component attribution to future work.
title: MemoHarness: Agent Harnesses That Learn from Experience
title_translated: MemoHarness：从经验中学习的智能体控制框架
summary_translated: 智能体控制框架是外部控制层，通过管理上下文、工具、编排、记忆、解码和输出处理，将基础大语言模型转化为可执行智能体。尽管控制框架设计显著影响智能体行为，但大多数自动优化方法仅针对提示、流水线或工作流等较窄对象，且部署后的智能体通常对所有场景复用单一全局框架。我们提出MemoHarness，一种从自身执行中学习的自适应框架优化框架。MemoHarness将控制框架分解为六个可编辑的控制维度，将每案例的诊断结果和提炼出的全局模式存储于双层经验库中，并在无需测试时标签、反馈或额外搜索的情况下，利用检索到的经验自适应调整每个测试案例的框架。在shell-agent、代码生成和分析推理基准上的评估表明，MemoHarness优于所对比的固定框架，并展现出对未见套件和基础模型的选择性迁移能力。其额外上下文开销在大部分检索经验可缓存时仍保持成本竞争力。这些结果表明，执行经验是构建比单一静态配置更具适应性的智能体框架的可行基础，而关于统计鲁棒性和组件归因的更广泛主张留待未来研究。

summary: This position paper explores how Agentic AI and Model Context Protocol (MCP) can support power-grid studies in a Transmission System Operator (TSO) context. We focus on integrating Large Language Models with numerical simulation tools, structured workflows, and human supervision. We identify key industrial requirements for agent assisted grid studies and introduce pypowsybl-mcp, an MCP-based interface exposing selected capabilities of our simulation tool, pypowsybl to AI agents. This first step provides a testbed to study how agents can setup simulations, execute analyses, retrieve results, and interact with power-system simulators through standardized tool calls. We also discuss principles for human-in-the-loop, multi-agent workflows and outline an evaluation strategy combining technical metrics and practitioner feedback. The paper positions MCP-based tool integration as a step toward more interactive, auditable, and scalable grid-study environments.
title: Orchestrating Power Grid Studies with Multi-Agent AI and MCP Servers
title_translated: 基于多智能体AI与MCP服务器的电网研究协同
summary_translated: 本文探讨了代理型人工智能（Agentic AI）与模型上下文协议（MCP）如何在输电系统运营商（TSO）背景下支持电网研究。我们聚焦于将大型语言模型与数值模拟工具、结构化工作流及人类监督相结合。我们识别了代理辅助电网研究的关键工业需求，并引入pypowsybl-mcp，一个基于MCP的接口，向AI代理暴露我们模拟工具pypowsybl的部分功能。这一步骤提供了一个试验平台，用于研究智能体如何通过标准化工具调用设置模拟、执行分析、获取结果并与电力系统模拟器交互。我们还讨论了人机协同、多智能体工作流的设计原则，并概述了结合技术指标与从业者反馈的评估策略。本文将基于MCP的工具集成定位为迈向更具交互性、可审计性和可扩展性的电网研究环境的重要一步。

summary: Type 1 Diabetes (T1D) is a chronic, life-threatening autoimmune condition characterized by the complete destruction of insulin-producing pancreatic beta cells. While Artificial Pancreas Systems (APS) powered by Reinforcement Learning (RL) have shown promise in automating insulin delivery, their ``black-box'' nature makes it hard for patients and doctors to trust them fully. This paper presents LLM-T1D, a promising approach that combines the precision of RL with the clear, human-like reasoning of Large Language Models (LLMs) to create a more transparent and reliable insulin pump controller. By training an expert RL system and distilling its knowledge into fine-tuned LLaMA 3.1 8B and Qwen3 8B models, we developed a controller that not only surpasses the RL system's performance but also explains its decisions in plain, understandable language. Tested on the FDA-approved UVA/Padova T1D simulator, the LLM controllers deliver excellent blood sugar control (73.5% Time in Range) while maintaining strict formal safety verification against hallucinations.
title: Interpretable Language Model for Closed-Loop Type 1 Diabetes Control
comment: Accepted at the 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering conference (IEEE CASE 2026)
title_translated: 用于闭环1型糖尿病控制的可解释语言模型
summary_translated: 1型糖尿病（T1D）是一种慢性、危及生命的自身免疫性疾病，特征是胰腺β细胞完全破坏，导致无法产生胰岛素。尽管由强化学习（RL）驱动的人工胰腺系统（APS）在自动化胰岛素输送方面已显示出潜力，但其“黑箱”特性使得患者和医生难以完全信任。本文提出LLM-T1D，一种结合RL精度与大语言模型（LLMs）清晰、类人推理能力的有前景方法，旨在创建更透明、可靠的胰岛素泵控制器。通过训练一个专家级RL系统，并将其知识蒸馏到微调后的LLaMA 3.1 8B和Qwen3 8B模型中，我们开发出的控制器不仅超越了RL系统的性能，还能以通俗易懂的语言解释其决策过程。在FDA批准的UVA/Padova T1D模拟器上测试，LLM控制器实现了优异的血糖控制效果（73.5%时间处于目标范围），同时通过严格的正式安全验证防止幻觉。

summary: Inspired by how humans communicate spatial information, language-guided geo-localization has gained significant traction for its intuitive and practical value. Despite this progress, most methods still rely on a static, one-shot retrieval paradigm, which fails to handle the ambiguity and incompleteness inherent in real-world natural language descriptions. We propose a paradigm shift to reasoning retrieval and introduce Dialogue Place Recognition (DlgPR), which casts localization as an interactive, dialogue-driven reasoning process. To support this new task, we present DlgQuest-Cities, the first large-scale dialogue-based benchmark for place recognition, and a unified reasoning framework that couples a cross-modal multi-level retriever with an intelligent questioner, DQ-pilot. DQ-pilot is trained in a curriculum: supervised fine-tuning on a curated DQ-cities-20k subset followed by reinforcement refinement on a harder DQ-cities-10k split via GRPO. Two task-aligned metrics guide learning: a Discriminative Difficulty Index (DDI) for curriculum sampling and a Positional Retrieval Gain (PRG) reward that directly measures retrieval improvement induced by a question. Experiments show this reasoning-based approach significantly outperforms baselines. The code and model are available at https://github.com/Graysonggg/DlgPR.
title: DialogueVPR: Towards Conversational Visual Place Recognition
title_translated: DialogueVPR：迈向对话式视觉定位
summary_translated: 受人类交流空间信息方式的启发，语言引导的地理定位因其直观性和实用性而受到广泛关注。尽管取得了进展，但大多数方法仍依赖于静态的一次性检索范式，无法有效处理现实世界自然语言描述中固有的模糊性和不完整性。我们提出一种范式转变，采用推理式检索，并引入对话式定位（DlgPR），将定位任务视为一种交互式的、由对话驱动的推理过程。为支持这一新任务，我们提出了DlgQuest-Cities，这是首个大规模基于对话的定位基准数据集，以及一个统一的推理框架，该框架将跨模态多层次检索器与智能提问者DQ-pilot相结合。DQ-pilot采用分阶段训练：先在精心筛选的DQ-cities-20k子集上进行监督微调，再通过GRPO在更具挑战性的DQ-cities-10k划分上进行强化学习优化。两项任务对齐的指标指导学习过程：用于课程采样的判别难度指数（DDI）和直接衡量问题带来的检索提升的定位检索增益（PRG）奖励。实验表明，这种基于推理的方法显著优于基线模型。代码和模型已公开于https://github.com/Graysonggg/DlgPR。
